zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Anne Pollard于1516年出生在英国戴文瓦也 白 阿申波頓时，她的父亲Anthony Pollard39岁，母亲Petronella Chudleigh34岁。她于大约1529年与Alexander Wollacombe esq在英国英格兰戴文结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世在英国英格兰戴文。

nameMeanings.name: Pollard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: perhaps a nickname from an unrecorded Middle English pollard, a derivative of the Middle English verb poll ‘to clip’ or the noun poll ‘the head’. It may have denoted someone with a close‐cropped head or a big head.
from the Middle English (Old French) personal name Pollard, a pet form of Paul (Old French Pol). The surname has been established in both England and Ireland since the 13th century.

variants.name: Paul
meanings: English, French, German, Dutch, Swedish, West Indian (mainly Haiti, also e.g. Saint Lucia), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the personal name Paul (from Latin Paulus ‘small’), which has always been popular in Christendom. It was the name adopted by the Pharisee Saul of Tarsus after his conversion to Christianity on the road to Damascus in about AD 34. He was a most energetic missionary to the Gentiles in the Roman Empire, and played a very significant role in establishing Christianity as a major world religion. The name was borne also by numerous other early Christian saints. It is also occasionally borne by Jews; the reasons for this are not clear. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages and their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Pavlis , Slovenian Pavel and Pavlič (see Pavlic ), Polish Paweł (see Pawel ) and Pawlicki , Assyrian/Chaldean Polous and Polus . In France, this surname is most common in Brittany (see 2 below).
Breton (mainly Finistère): from a Frenchified form of the personal name Paol, Breton form of Paul .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phóil ‘son of Paul’. Compare McFall .

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Anne Pollard was born in 1516, in Waye by Ashburton, Devon, England, her father, Anthony Pollard, was 39 and her mother, Petronella Chudleigh, was 34. She married Alexander Wollacombe esq about 1529, in Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in Devon, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Anne Pollard est née en 1516, à Waye by Ashburton, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Anthony Pollard, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Petronella Chudleigh, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé Alexander Wollacombe esq environ 1529, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Anne Pollard 1516 in Waye by Ashburton, Devon, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Anthony Pollard 39 und ihre Mutter Petronella Chudleigh 34. Sie hat um 1529 in Devon, England, United Kingdom Alexander Wollacombe esq geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb in Devon, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Anne Pollard è nata il nel 1516 nel Waye by Ashburton, Devon, England, sua padre, Anthony Pollard, aveva 39 anni e sua madre, Petronella Chudleigh, aveva 34 anni. Lei sposato Alexander Wollacombe esq circa 1529, nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Anne Pollard(이)가 1516년 영국 데폰 웨이 비 애스h버턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Anthony Pollard(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Petronella Chudleigh(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 대략 1529년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 Alexander Wollacombe esq(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Anne Pollard родилась в 1516 г. в местности веи би ашбуртон, девон, Англия, её отцу по имени Anthony Pollard было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Petronella Chudleigh, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Alexander Wollacombe esq около 1529 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания.
